channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
item.description: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model for on-device function calling, enabling efficient offline AI tasks on mobile devices. This innovation allows users to manage calendars and control device hardware with low latency, enhancing user experience significantly.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now integrates with various third-party tools like GitHub and Notion, allowing developers to create more capable AI agents. This ecosystem aims to enhance the functionality and real-world application of AI agents.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 will take place on May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This event aims to engage attendees and highlight advancements in AI technology.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas emphasizes in-person networking and hands-on problem solving, featuring sessions on agentic AI and Gemini multimodal breakthroughs. The event aims to equip developers with essential tools for modern challenges.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, a successor to TFLite, enhancing GPU and NPU acceleration and supporting PyTorch and JAX. This update positions LiteRT as a key framework for deploying advanced AI applications.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new weekly session by Google Cloud experts aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on building. This initiative focuses on practical applications in AI and developer productivity.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to improve coding workflows with tools like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These enhancements aim to make AI a more effective collaborator tailored to developers' needs.

item.title: Microsoft Purview: Purview in Microsoft Admin Center
item.description: Microsoft Purview in the Admin Center helps AI and IT admins manage oversharing risks and secure M365 Copilot interactions. This feature enhances data protection and compliance for organizations using AI tools.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: Annotations on Teams Single Window Sharing on macOS
item.description: Microsoft Teams has introduced a feature allowing presenters to share a single application window with annotation capabilities for participants. This enhances privacy and engagement during presentations.

item.title: Microsoft Viva: Viva Glint and Viva Insights Integration - Exclusion List for data sharing
item.description: Viva Glint now allows admins to manage an exclusion list for data shared with Viva Insights, enhancing privacy controls. This feature aims to streamline data management and ensure compliance with privacy standards.

item.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Loss Prevention – Inline DLP for Prompts for Microsoft Foundry apps and agents
item.description: Microsoft Purview introduces inline Data Loss Prevention (DLP) for prompts in Foundry apps, enhancing data security for sensitive information. This feature is set to be generally available by June 2026.

item.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Loss Prevention – Block External Domain/User action in DLP for SharePoint/OneDrive
item.description: Microsoft Purview enhances DLP for SharePoint and OneDrive by allowing admins to block access for specific external domains or users, improving control over sensitive files. This feature will be available in June 2026.

item.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show
item.description: OpenAI has acquired TBPN, a popular tech podcast, which will continue to operate independently under the leadership of Chris Lehane. This move expands OpenAI's media presence.

item.title: PSA: Anyone with a link can view your Granola notes by default
item.description: Users of the Granola note-taking app should review privacy settings, as notes are viewable by anyone with a link by default. The app also uses notes for AI training unless opted out.

item.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
item.description: New Rowhammer attacks, GDDRHammer and GeForge, exploit vulnerabilities in Nvidia GPUs, allowing attackers to gain complete control over affected machines. This raises concerns about hardware security.

item.title: Microsoft takes on AI rivals with three new foundational models
item.description: Microsoft has launched three new foundational AI models capable of transcribing voice to text and generating audio and images, marking a significant step in its AI development.

item.title: Google now lets you direct avatars through prompts in its Vids app
item.description: Google's Vids app now allows users to direct avatars through prompts, enhancing customization and interactivity in video creation.

item.title: Elon Musk is about to be a very busy boy!
item.description: Elon Musk is expected to file for SpaceX's IPO soon, with June 7th being a potential date, indicating significant developments for the company.

item.title: OpenAI just bought TBPN
item.description: OpenAI has officially purchased TBPN, a talk show that features interviews with AI and tech leaders, further integrating itself into the tech media landscape.

item.title: 5 top SOC-as-a-service providers and how to evaluate them
item.description: This article reviews five top SOC-as-a-service providers, focusing on key evaluation criteria like tools, automation, and compliance to simplify security operations.

item.title: cc-context-stats 1.15.1
item.description: The release of cc-context-stats 1.15.1 allows users to monitor their Claude Code session context in real-time, tracking token usage effectively. This tool helps prevent running out of context during sessions.

item.title: It’s not easy to get depression-detecting AI through the FDA
item.description: Kintsugi, a startup focused on AI for detecting depression and anxiety, is shutting down after failing to secure FDA approval. The company plans to release most of its technology as open-source, potentially benefiting future developments.

item.title: When the machine asks you to stay
item.description: Sam Altman's promise of allowing verified adults to access erotica through ChatGPT has sparked significant online discussion. This move is framed as a step towards treating adults with respect in digital spaces.

item.title: yantrix-adapters 1.0.0
item.description: Yantrix-adapters 1.0.0 introduces cross-framework adapters for various agent infrastructures, enhancing compatibility and functionality across platforms like LangChain and AutoGen.

item.title: yantrix-adapters added to PyPI
item.description: The yantrix-adapters have been added to PyPI, providing developers with tools for cross-framework compatibility in AI applications.

item.title: Microsoft just had its worst quarter on Wall Street in nearly two decades — the steepest fall since the Great Recession
item.description: Microsoft reported its worst quarterly performance in nearly two decades, primarily due to costly AI investments that have yet to yield significant returns. This raises concerns about the sustainability of such investments.

item.title: scrapingbee-cli 1.4.0
item.description: The scrapingbee-cli 1.4.0 is a command-line client for the ScrapingBee API, allowing users to scrape web pages and access various services directly from the terminal.

item.title: Mozilla President Mark Surman on AI, the future of the open web, and collapsing publisher models
item.description: Mozilla President Mark Surman discusses the transformative impact of AI on the internet and the potential for redefining the open web amidst changing publisher models.

item.title: OpenAI's CFO says the company is passing on opportunities because it does not have enough compute
item.description: OpenAI's CFO Sarah Friar revealed that the company is forgoing certain opportunities due to insufficient computing resources, indicating a strategic focus on resource allocation.

item.title: pytorch-geometric added to PyPI
item.description: A dummy package named pytorch-geometric has been added to PyPI to assist users who mistakenly install non-existent package names, addressing a common issue in the community.

item.title: pytorch-geometric 0.0.2
item.description: A new dummy package named pytorch-geometric 0.0.2 has been created to assist users who mistakenly install non-existent packages suggested by LLMs. This highlights the issue of LLM hallucinations in software development.

item.title: The Toolkit Pattern
item.description: This article discusses the toolkit pattern in AI-driven development, focusing on how to document project configurations effectively. It is part of a series exploring agentic engineering.

item.title: Show HN: Hacker News, summarized and printed to your local printer
item.description: A new tool allows users to summarize Hacker News articles and print them locally. This tool aims to enhance accessibility and engagement with online content.

item.title: videosdk-plugins-openai 1.0.2
item.description: The videosdk-plugins-openai 1.0.2 plugin has been released, enabling integration of OpenAI services within the VideoSDK Agent Framework. This expands the capabilities of video applications using AI.

item.title: videosdk-plugins-langchain 1.0.2
item.description: The videosdk-plugins-langchain 1.0.2 plugin has been introduced, allowing integration of LangChain and LangGraph into the VideoSDK Agent Framework, enhancing its versatility.

item.title: Big Tech Shouldn’t Be Writing the Rules for AI
item.description: Anthropic's conflict with the Trump administration highlights the need for governments to take responsibility in AI governance, rather than leaving it to big tech companies. This raises concerns about corporate influence on AI regulations.

item.title: Blend’s post-IPO reset: CEO Nima Ghamsari bets that AI can turn it all around
item.description: Blend's CEO Nima Ghamsari is pivoting the company towards AI-driven automation following a downturn in the mortgage market. This shift aims to revitalize the company's performance post-IPO.

item.title: The Dynamic Island is Finally Changing: New iPhone 18 Leak Reveals 35% Smaller Design
item.description: Recent leaks about the iPhone 18 suggest a 35% smaller Dynamic Island design, indicating a focus on internal advancements rather than a complete redesign. This could significantly impact user experience.

item.title: chem_mat_database 1.7.1
item.description: The chem_mat_database 1.7.1 has been released, providing a Command Line Interface for various projects. This tool aims to streamline project management in chemistry-related applications.

item.title: AI laws overlook environmental damage – here’s what needs to change
item.description: The article discusses the need to integrate environmental considerations into AI laws to mitigate potential ecological damage as AI technology expands rapidly. It emphasizes a balanced approach to AI governance.

item.title: Jack Tan, Zizan Razak Join 2026 Ambassador Lineup as Malaysia Film Fest Unveils ‘Resonance’ Theme
item.description: The 9th Malaysia International Film Festival has announced its 2026 ambassadors, including actors Zizan Razak and Jack Tan, alongside key programming details. The festival will take place from July 18 to 25 in Kuala Lumpur, highlighting the importance of cultural events in the region.

item.title: hcom 0.7.8
item.description: The hcom 0.7.8 update allows AI agents to communicate and interact across terminals, enhancing capabilities for Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex, and OpenCode. This development signifies a step forward in AI agent collaboration.

item.title: Maintaining cyber control when AI can act autonomously
item.description: This article discusses the importance of defining the permissions and workflows for autonomous AI agents to ensure they operate within authorized boundaries. It raises critical questions about governance and control in AI systems.

item.title: litert-torch-nightly 0.9.0.dev20260402
item.description: The litert-torch-nightly 0.9.0.dev20260402 update introduces support for converting PyTorch models with LiteRT, enhancing model deployment efficiency. This update is significant for developers working with AI models.

item.title: Freight Technologies Announces Strategic Update on Transition to AI-Powered, Software-First Logistics Technology Company
item.description: Freight Technologies is exploring strategic options for its online brokerage business while accelerating the commercialization of its AI-powered logistics platform. This move indicates a shift towards a software-first approach in logistics.

item.title: evaluatorq 1.2.3rc1
item.description: The evaluatorq 1.2.3rc1 update introduces a flexible evaluation framework for Python, allowing parallel evaluations and integration with the Orq AI platform. This tool is beneficial for developers in AI research and application.

item.title: GitHub Will Use Copilot Interaction Data from Free, Pro, and Pro+ Users to Train AI Models
item.description: GitHub plans to use interaction data from Copilot users to train its AI models, starting April 24. This data collection will include various user interactions, raising privacy and ethical concerns.

item.title: iPhone 18 Pro Reportedly Won't Come in Black
item.description: The upcoming iPhone 18 Pro will reportedly not be available in black, continuing a trend seen with the iPhone 17 Pro. This change reflects Apple's evolving design choices and market strategy.

item.title: torchax 0.0.12.dev20260402
item.description: The torchax 0.0.12.dev20260402 update introduces a library for running Jax and PyTorch together, enhancing flexibility for developers working with different AI frameworks. This integration is significant for AI model development.

item.title: UAT-10608: Inside a large-scale automated credential harvesting operation targeting web applications
item.description: Talos has disclosed a large-scale automated credential harvesting operation, tracked as UAT-10608, which uses a framework called 'NEXUS Listener.' This campaign highlights ongoing cybersecurity threats targeting web applications.
